claim_boundary.scope: Request manifest only. It packages selected h10 cells, native stagger context, patch bounds, full-column requirements, and WRF term groups for future CPU-WRF instrumentation.

environment.note: This script uses only the Python standard library and does not import JAX.

indexing_contract.fortran_translation: for a zero-based [start, stop_exclusive) range, instrument WRF with 1-based inclusive [start+1, stop_exclusive]
indexing_contract.manifest_indices: zero-based Python indices with stop-exclusive patch bounds

required_companion_artifacts: instrumentation patch diff or commit hash
exact run command and namelist files
short manifest echo proving these 24 cells and h10 were used

/stage_<rk_stage>/final_stage_state: end-step boundary/spec-relax before/after arrays where applied
/stage_<rk_stage>/large_step/<term_group: named term arrays on native staggering
routine input arrays needed to reproduce each term
/stage_<rk_stage>/stage_input: boundary leaves active at stage entry

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: stage input
requested_terms: QVAPOR and other active moisture scalars
boundary/spec-relax leaves active for the sampled step
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: before each RK stage tendency assembly

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: mass coupling
requested_terms: coupled velocity intermediates used by advection

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: momentum advection
requested_terms: momentum flux components and final advection tendency arrays

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: scalar/theta/mu advection
requested_terms: QVAPOR and active scalar advection tendencies
scalar flux components used by the sampled configuration

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: diffusion
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: large-step diffusion according to active diff_opt/km_opt

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: horizontal PGF
requested_terms: named pressure-gradient pieces before final sum where available

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: Coriolis
requested_terms: vertical coupling terms or rw_cor if present/active

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: source-tendency folding
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: before and after WRF folds external/source tendencies into RK tendencies

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: small-step prep
requested_terms: time weights and RK weights used by the acoustic step
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: after large-step tendency assembly, before acoustic scan

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: acoustic U/V
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: requested first and last acoustic substeps for each RK stage

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: MU/theta
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: requested first and last acoustic substeps for each RK stage

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: W/PH
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: requested first and last acoustic substeps for each RK stage

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: pressure/rho refresh
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: after MU/theta/W/PH updates inside acoustic path and at final stage reconstruction

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: boundary/spec-relax
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: before and after in-loop normal boundary work and end-step lateral boundary application

requested_wrf_source_term_groups.name: final stage state
requested_terms: surface diagnostics if already produced by WRF at this point
requested_wrf_source_term_groups.timing: after each RK stage and after the final stage carry/finish

acoustic_substep_samples.note: Record for every requested RK stage.
Record for every requested RK stage; de-duplicate if n_acoustic_substeps == 1.

same_state_sampling_request.model_step: Instrument one real CPU-WRF large timestep at the h10 same-state target. Record the exact WRF model step number, WRF Times value, dt, and namelist switches; do not infer cadence from hourly output alone.
stage_boundaries: post large-step total tendencies before acoustic

candidate_filters.fallback: outside 8-cell frame, then any finite mass-grid cell
candidate_filters.preferred: outside 8-cell frame and LANDMASK == 0 or HGT < 300 m

selection.candidate_source: highest primary localization score in h10-h14; fallback h8-h14/all leads if unavailable
selection.full_column_reason: The listed levels are reporting probes only. WRF savepoints must write full native vertical columns for every selected cell/face because horizontal stencils, vertical coupling, pressure/geopotential refreshes, W/PH updates, and acoustic work arrays can depend on levels outside the first-probe list.

expected_first_target.reason: h10 is the strongest selected h8-h14 divergence lead in the dynamic attribution plan.

wrf_source_build_feasibility_dependency.fastest_reliable_path: Use a disposable instrumented copy of /home/enric/src/wrf_pristine/WRF, because it has a current WRF v4.7.1 source tree and built CPU executables. Run the Case 3 d01/d02 forecast from 2026-05-01_18:00:00 to h10 with a small env-gated Fortran emitter that dumps selected d02 cell patches around existing dycore routine boundaries. Do not use the old external/wrf_savepoint_patch as-is; its hooks are empty and its build paths are stale.
wrf_source_build_feasibility_dependency.impact: Use this feasibility proof for WRF source/build choice and provenance risks. The selected h10 cells, levels, and patch bounds in this request remain sourced from dynamic_field_attribution.json.
wrf_source_build_feasibility_dependency.source_truth_quality: source-derived WRF term savepoints are feasible, but the first implementation sprint must record build provenance and validate step/lead mapping before accepting h10 term data.
